key_insight: AI is turning nanoparticle electron microscopy from image interpretation into scientific inference by quantifying structure, defects, dynamics, and synthesis-property links across TEM, HRTEM, STEM, and in situ data.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more automated nanoparticle characterization and supports closed-loop materials discovery by connecting microscopy outputs to simulation, metadata, and experimental control.
method: Conventional ML and CNNs for detection, segmentation, and morphology quantification
Transformers, self-supervised learning, and foundation models for restoration, defect finding, and multimodal inference
Physics-informed learning and autonomous microscopy for linking images with dynamics, structure, and function
evidence_signal: The review synthesizes methods across multiple microscopy modalities and evaluates them by their data requirements, benchmarking status, strengths, and limitations.
watch_item: Performance can be limited by scarce labeled data, modality-specific benchmarking gaps, and the risk of unreliable inference if physical constraints and metadata are not incorporated.
paper_title: The evolution of AI from image interpretation toward scientific inference in nanoparticle electron microscopy
